an10.79:1.1:
"Bhikkhus, there are these ten grounds for resentment. What ten? (1)
‘They acted for my harm,’ one harbors resentment. (2)
‘They are acting for my harm,’ one harbors resentment. (3)
‘They will act for my harm,’ one harbors resentment. (4)
‘They acted for the harm of one who is pleasing and agreeable to me,’ one harbors resentment. (5)
‘They are acting for the harm of one who is pleasing and agreeable to me,’ one harbors resentment. (6)
‘They will act for the harm of one who is pleasing and agreeable to me,’ one harbors resentment. (7)
‘They acted for the benefit of one who is displeasing and disagreeable to me,’ one harbors resentment. (8)
‘They are acting for the benefit of one who is displeasing and disagreeable to me,’ one harbors resentment. (9)
‘They will act for the benefit of one who is displeasing and disagreeable to me,’ one harbors resentment. (10) And one becomes angry without a reason.
These, bhikkhus, are the ten grounds for resentment."
